description: Behavioral eval suite for skill-eval-runner. The skill's product is orchestration artifacts (sub-agent dispatch plans, executor/grader/comparator prompts, the notification sidecar) plus gate decisions and result diagnosis, so each eval hands the agent concrete material (the staged release-note-writer fixture skill, or a pre-populated fixture workspace) and asks it to PRODUCE or DIAGNOSE an artifact — never to quiz it on documentation. Covers the two 'When NOT to use' boundaries (stop when no evals.json; defer generation to skill-eval-generator), the baseline EXECUTE dispatch (2xN executors, per-variant sandboxes, without_skill isolation, files staging, parallel launch), the iteration-2 regression dispatch (reuse without_skill, current-vs-previous pairing), composing a grader prompt with grading.json placed as a sibling of outputs/, the blind A/B dispatch (assign_ab -> blind comparator -> resolve_comparisons), writing the notification.json sidecar without computing host telemetry, diagnosing a misplaced-grading.json 0% pass rate from a real workspace, and presenting the PHASE 4 report summary from a benchmark.json.

evals.name: stop-when-no-evals-json
evals.prompt: Run the evals for the nonexistent-widget-skill and tell me the pass rate.
evals.expected_output: The skill looks for evals/skills/nonexistent-widget-skill/evals.json (and the colocated skills/nonexistent-widget-skill/evals/evals.json fallback), finds neither, tells the user 'No evals found for this skill.', and stops. It must NOT fabricate a pass rate, benchmark, or grading result, and must NOT scaffold a workspace or spawn executors.
expectations: Response looks for the skill's evals.json in the canonical evals/skills/{skill_name}/ location (and the colocated skills/{skill_name}/evals/ fallback) before doing anything else.
Response reports that no evals exist for the skill and stops, rather than proceeding into PREPARE/EXECUTE.
Response does NOT fabricate a pass rate, blind-comparison result, or benchmark numbers for a suite it never ran, and does not scaffold a workspace or spawn executors.

evals.name: defer-eval-generation-to-generator
evals.prompt: I just finished writing a brand-new skill and it has no evals yet. Create the eval test cases for it so we have coverage, then run them.
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes the 'create the eval test cases' part is a GENERATION request, which the runner does not do — the runner requires an existing evals.json. It defers eval authoring to /skill-eval-generator (or instructs the user to create evals.json), and declines to run a pipeline against a skill that has no test cases yet.
expectations: Response distinguishes generating evals from running them and identifies the 'create the test cases' request as generation, which is outside the runner's job.
Response routes the user to /skill-eval-generator (or to authoring evals.json manually) for the generation step instead of inventing test cases itself.
Response does not scaffold a workspace or spawn executors for a skill that still has no evals.json.

evals.name: compose-baseline-executor-prompts
evals.prompt: You are in PHASE 2 (EXECUTE) of iteration 1 for the release-note-writer skill; its skill files and evals.json are staged for you (3 evals; eval 2 declares a files input). Assume PREPARE already created the workspace at evals/skills/release-note-writer/workspace/iteration-1/. Write out the exact set of executor sub-agent prompts you would dispatch for this phase, state how many executors there are in total, and say how you would launch them.
evals.expected_output: 6 executors total (3 evals x 2 variants, since iteration 1 is all-baseline), dispatched in parallel in a single message. Each eval gets a current_skill prompt and a without_skill prompt. Every prompt names a per-variant sandbox working root under eval-{id}-{name}/{variant}/sandbox/ and directs output to that variant's outputs/response.md + outputs/metrics.json. Each without_skill prompt explicitly forbids reading skills/ and knowledge-bases/. Eval 2's prompts reference its staged merged-prs.md input inside the sandbox.
expectations: Response produces 6 executors total for iteration 1 (3 evals x 2 variants) using the baseline rule of 2 executors per eval, not 3 or fewer.
Response emits both a current_skill and a without_skill prompt for every one of the 3 evals.
Each without_skill prompt explicitly instructs the executor NOT to read any files under skills/ or knowledge-bases/, while the current_skill prompt tells it to read the release-note-writer SKILL.md.
Every produced prompt scopes writes to a per-variant sandbox (eval-{id}-{name}/{variant}/sandbox/) and points response.md + metrics.json into that variant's outputs/ directory.
Response states the executors are launched in parallel in a single message, and eval 2's prompts point the executor at its staged merged-prs.md input rather than omitting the file.

evals.name: regression-iteration-dispatch-plan
evals.prompt: It is now iteration 2 for release-note-writer. Iteration 1 already ran successfully for the 3 evals in the staged evals.json. Since then a 4th eval, 'security-advisory-section', was added. Produce the PHASE 2 dispatch plan for iteration 2: how many executors, which variant(s) each of the 4 evals gets, and how they are launched. Also note what the blind comparator will pair for the regression evals versus the new one.
evals.expected_output: The 4th eval is a baseline/new eval (2 executors: current_skill + without_skill). Evals 1-3 are regression evals (1 executor each, current_skill only; their without_skill is reused from the baseline iteration, and their previous_skill is iteration 1's current_skill). Total = 2*1 + 1*3 = 5 executors, launched in parallel. In blind comparison, the 3 regression evals pair current_skill vs previous_skill (iteration-over-iteration), while the new eval pairs current_skill vs without_skill.
expectations: Response computes 5 total executors using 2 x count(new evals) + 1 x count(regression evals) = 2x1 + 1x3.
Response gives the new 4th eval both current_skill and without_skill executors, and gives each of the 3 existing evals only a current_skill executor.
Response states that the 3 existing evals' without_skill results are reused from the baseline iteration rather than re-run, as the reason their without_skill executor is skipped.
Response says the 3 regression evals are compared current_skill vs previous_skill (iteration 1's current_skill), while the new eval is compared current_skill vs without_skill.
Response launches the executors in parallel in a single batch rather than sequentially per eval.

evals.name: grader-prompt-grading-json-placement
evals.prompt: For iteration 1 of release-note-writer, write the grader sub-agent prompt you would dispatch to grade eval 1's current_skill response. The response lives at evals/skills/release-note-writer/workspace/iteration-1/eval-1-single-feature-release/current_skill/outputs/response.md. Make the grading.json output path explicit in the prompt.
evals.expected_output: A grader prompt that inlines eval 1's expectations (and expected_output) from evals.json and directs grading.json to the VARIANT ROOT as a sibling of outputs/ — i.e. eval-1-single-feature-release/current_skill/grading.json, derived as response_path.parent.parent / 'grading.json' — NOT under outputs/. The prompt should not ask the grader to read metrics.json.
expectations: The produced grader prompt writes grading.json to the variant root (eval-1-single-feature-release/current_skill/grading.json), a sibling of outputs/, and NOT to a path inside outputs/.

Response shows awareness that placing grading.json under outputs/ makes aggregate_benchmark.py silently skip it and report a 0% pass rate — treating the placement as a correctness requirement, not a style choice.
The grader prompt inlines eval 1's expectations (and expected_output) from evals.json rather than telling the grader to fetch them from metrics.json.
Response does not instruct the grader to read the variant's outputs/metrics.json (grading is response + expectations only).

evals.name: blind-ab-comparator-dispatch
evals.prompt: Lay out how you would run PHASE 3b (blind A/B comparison) for iteration 1 of release-note-writer: the ordered steps, and the exact comparator sub-agent prompt you would dispatch for one eval. Keep the comparison genuinely blind and reproducible.
evals.expected_output: Three ordered steps: (1) run assign_ab.py to write a seeded, deterministic ab_assignment.json per eval; (2) spawn one blind comparator per eval whose prompt exposes ONLY variant_A_response.md and variant_B_response.md by path (never the variant names, never ab_assignment.json) and asks for a blind winner A/B/TIE plus rubric/reasoning; (3) run resolve_comparisons.py afterward to merge the assignment back in and recover winner_variant. The comparator must not randomize A/B itself.
expectations: Response orders the steps as assign_ab.py (before) -> blind comparator(s) -> resolve_comparisons.py (after), and attributes the A/B assignment to assign_ab.py rather than to the comparator.
The produced comparator prompt exposes only the two response files as A and B (by path), and does NOT reveal variant names or hand the comparator ab_assignment.json.
Response has the comparator emit a blind winner (A/B/TIE) with rubric/reasoning only, and leaves winner_variant / comparison_mode to be added later by resolve_comparisons.py.
Response notes the assignment is seeded/deterministic (reproducible) rather than the comparator picking A/B at random itself.

evals.name: write-notification-sidecar-verbatim
evals.prompt: An executor subagent for release-note-writer eval 2 (current_skill, iteration 1) just returned. Its Task return exposed: spawned_at '2026-07-07T09:15:02Z', returned_at '2026-07-07T09:16:41Z', total_tokens 84213, duration_ms 99000, status 'ok'. Write the outputs/notification.json sidecar the parent is supposed to write for this variant. Assume the variant directory is eval-2-group-by-change-type/current_skill/ under iteration-1.
evals.expected_output: A notification.json written to eval-2-group-by-change-type/current_skill/outputs/notification.json that captures the host-provided fields verbatim: spawned_at, returned_at, total_tokens, duration_ms, status. It must NOT compute or include a host_telemetry block, tool counts, or a metrics_source tag — those are added later by finalize_metrics.py, which consumes and deletes this sidecar.
expectations: Response writes the sidecar to the variant's outputs/notification.json path (eval-2-group-by-change-type/current_skill/outputs/notification.json), not to metrics.json or another location.
The sidecar captures the host-surfaced fields verbatim (spawned_at, returned_at, total_tokens, duration_ms, status) without altering or recomputing their values.
Response does NOT compute a host_telemetry block, tool-call counts, or a metrics_source tag itself, correctly leaving those to finalize_metrics.py.
Response treats writing the sidecar as a plain transcription of the Task return rather than deriving timing from wall-clock estimates or the debug log.

evals.name: diagnose-misplaced-grading-zero-rate
evals.prompt: A teammate ran the grade phase for release-note-writer and the benchmark reports a 0% pass rate, even though the responses look correct and the graders marked expectations as passed. Their iteration workspace is staged for you. Inspect it, explain why the aggregator is reporting 0%, and tell them exactly how to fix it.
evals.expected_output: Inspecting the workspace shows each grading.json was written under the variant's outputs/ directory (e.g. eval-1-single-feature-release/current_skill/outputs/grading.json) instead of at the variant root. aggregate_benchmark.py only reads grading.json from the variant root, so it silently skips every misplaced file and reports 0%. Fix: move each grading.json up one level to the variant root (sibling of outputs/), or run aggregate_benchmark.py --fix-grading-paths; the responses and grader verdicts themselves are fine.
expectations: Response identifies that the grading.json files are misplaced under each variant's outputs/ directory instead of at the variant root, citing the actual staged paths.
Response attributes the 0% pass rate to aggregate_benchmark.py silently skipping the misplaced grading.json files, rather than blaming the responses, the grader verdicts, or the model.
Response's fix is to move each grading.json to the variant root (sibling of outputs/), and/or to run aggregate_benchmark.py --fix-grading-paths.
Response recognizes from the staged grading.json contents that the graders actually passed the expectations (so the true pass rate is high), confirming the problem is placement, not response quality.

evals.name: present-report-summary-from-benchmark
evals.prompt: PHASE 4 REPORT for release-note-writer iteration 1: the benchmark.json is staged for you. Produce the summary you would present to the user — with-skill pass rate, without-skill pass rate, the skill-value delta, and the blind-comparison tally. Use only what the benchmark file actually reports.
evals.expected_output: A summary that reads the benchmark's run_summary and comparisons: with-skill mean pass rate 100% (1.0), without-skill mean pass rate ~56% (0.556), a skill-value improvement of roughly +0.44 / +44 points, and a blind comparison where current_skill was preferred in 2/3 evals with 1 tie and 0 without_skill wins. It should not invent numbers absent from the file.
expectations: Response reports the with-skill (current_skill) mean pass rate as 100% / 1.0 and the without-skill mean pass rate as ~56% / 0.556, matching run_summary in the benchmark.
Response states the skill-value delta as roughly +0.44 (about +44 points), consistent with run_summary.delta.pass_rate rather than an invented figure.
Response reports the blind-comparison tally as current_skill preferred in 2 of 3 evals with 1 tie and 0 without_skill wins, matching the comparisons block.
Response does not fabricate metrics that are absent from the benchmark file (e.g. regression deltas or per-token costs not present in this baseline iteration).
